URL with extra query params 6. Invalid/malformed URL 7. Non-Drive URL ## Test Cases for URL builders 1. Valid file ID produces correct URL structure 2. Empty file ID handled gracefully ## File scripts/tests/google-drive.test.mjs ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:59.843548866-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:54:40.612603432-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T02:54:40.612603432-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 20 tests for extractGoogleDriveFileId, getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl, getGoogleDriveViewerUrl in google-drive.test.mjs. Commit 03a9365.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-54j","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:59.845287008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-84w","title":"Task: Unit tests for EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)","description":"## What Test getExifDateTime() function that extracts datetime from image EXIF metadata. ## Function Behavior Returns ISO 8601 datetime string or null if not found. ## Test Cases 1. JPEG with DateTimeOriginal field 2. JPEG with only DateTimeDigitized (fallback) 3. JPEG with only DateTime (fallback) 4. JPEG with no datetime fields 5. PNG (no EXIF support typically) 6. HEIC with datetime 7. Corrupt/truncated buffer 8. Empty buffer ## Test Fixtures Required - scripts/tests/fixtures/images/sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg - Create with known datetime for deterministic test ## File scripts/tests/exif-datetime.test.mjs ## Note Uses 'exifr' library internally - tests should use real image buffers ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:38.201002957-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:38.201002957-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-84w","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:38.202679253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-8x5","title":"Task: E2E test for Google Drive platform (experimental)","description":"## What Create E2E integration test for Google Drive downloads. Marked as [experimental]. ## Test Script scripts/tests/e2e/google-drive.test.sh ## Prerequisites - Need a stable Google Drive test URL (shared as 'Anyone with link') - Store expected SHA256 in fixtures ## Test Steps 1. Download from drive.google.com/file/d/.../view URL 2. Verify file downloaded (may be lower resolution) 3. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'gdrive') 4. Log any auth detection ## Verbose Logging ``` [TIME] [INFO] Platform: gdrive [EXPERIMENTAL] [TIME] [INFO] URL: https://drive.google.com/file/d/... [TIME] [INFO] Attempting direct download... [TIME] [WARN] Direct download failed, trying thumbnail extraction [TIME] [INFO] Downloaded via thumbnail: 1920x1080 (thumbnail quality) [TIME] [WARN] Full resolution not available (expected for Drive) [TIME] [PASS] Downloaded successfully with fallback ``` ## Error Scenario Test Also test with a NON-public file to verify auth detection: ``` [TIME] [INFO] Testing auth detection... [TIME] [INFO] Using restricted test URL [TIME] [FAIL] Exit code 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) - expected [TIME] [PASS] Auth detection working correctly ``` ## Notes - Google Drive is experimental - quality may vary - Auth detection is critical to avoid capturing login pages - Test should pass even if resolution is degraded ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:54.689912912-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:54.689912912-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-8x5","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-fmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:54.72077487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-8x5","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:54.725886032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","title":"FEATURE: Test Infrastructure - Logging, Fixtures, CI Pipeline","description":"## Overview Build robust test infrastructure that supports both unit and E2E tests with: - Verbose structured logging for CI debugging - Real test fixtures (actual image files, not mocks) - CI pipeline enhancements - Local development convenience scripts ## Logging Framework ### Requirements - JSON-structured logs for CI parsing - Human-readable format for local dev - Timing metrics for performance regression detection - Clear PASS/FAIL status per test ### Implementation ```bash # scripts/tests/lib/log.sh log_info() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >&2; } log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" >&2; } log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" >&2; } log_json() { echo "{\"ts\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"level\":\"$1\",\"msg\":\"$2\"}" >&2; } ``` ## Test Fixtures ### scripts/tests/fixtures/ ``` fixtures/ ├── images/ │ ├── sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg # Real JPEG with EXIF datetime │ ├── sample-png.png # Basic PNG │ ├── sample-heic.heic # HEIC for conversion tests │ └── sample-webp.webp # WebP format ├── magic-bytes/ │ ├── jpeg-magic.bin # First 12 bytes of JPEG │ ├── html-magic.bin # HTML document start │ └── heic-magic.bin # HEIC ftyp header └── expected-hashes/ ├── icloud.sha256 ├── dropbox.sha256 └── google-photos.sha256 ``` ### Source for Fixtures - Use royalty-free test images - Generate minimal valid images programmatically where possible - Store actual EXIF data for datetime extraction tests ## CI Pipeline Enhancements ### .github/workflows/ci.yml additions ```yaml # Add after existing unit-tests job e2e-tests: name: E2E Integration Tests needs: [shellcheck, syntax-check, unit-tests] runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 30 strategy: fail-fast: false matrix: platform: [icloud, dropbox, google-photos] steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Setup dependencies run: ... - name: Run ${{ matrix.platform }} test run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/${{ matrix.platform }}.test.sh env: GIIL_LOG_FORMAT: json ``` ## Local Development Scripts ### scripts/tests/run-all.sh ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash # Run all tests: unit + e2e echo '=== Unit Tests ===' ./scripts/tests/run-tests.sh echo '=== E2E Tests ===' for test in ./scripts/tests/e2e/*.test.sh; do echo "--- $(basename $test) ---" bash "$test" done ``` ### scripts/tests/run-quick.sh ```bash # Fast tests only (unit tests, skip live E2E) node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs ``` ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:01.784720427-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:17:40.350703412-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:17:40.350703412-05:00","close_reason":"Test infrastructure complete: logging framework (lib/log.sh), fixtures directory with magic bytes, run-quick.sh and run-all.sh convenience scripts, CI pipeline E2E test matrix. Unblocks i8w, 3ry, and downstream E2E tests."} +{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","title":"FEATURE: Test Infrastructure - Logging, Fixtures, CI Pipeline","description":"## Overview Build robust test infrastructure that supports both unit and E2E tests with: - Verbose structured logging for CI debugging - Real test fixtures (actual image files, not mocks) - CI pipeline enhancements - Local development convenience scripts ## Logging Framework ### Requirements - JSON-structured logs for CI parsing - Human-readable format for local dev - Timing metrics for performance regression detection - Clear PASS/FAIL status per test ### Implementation ```bash # scripts/tests/lib/log.sh log_info() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >&2; } log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" >&2; } log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" >&2; } log_json() { echo "{\"ts\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"level\":\"$1\",\"msg\":\"$2\"}" >&2; } ``` ## Test Fixtures ### scripts/tests/fixtures/ ``` fixtures/ ├── images/ │ ├── sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg # Real JPEG with EXIF datetime │ ├── sample-png.png # Basic PNG │ ├── sample-heic.heic # HEIC for conversion tests │ └── sample-webp.webp # WebP format ├── magic-bytes/ │ ├── jpeg-magic.bin # First 12 bytes of JPEG │ ├── html-magic.bin # HTML document start │ └── heic-magic.bin # HEIC ftyp header └── expected-hashes/ ├── icloud.sha256 ├── dropbox.sha256 └── google-photos.sha256 ``` ### Source for Fixtures - Use royalty-free test images - Generate minimal valid images programmatically where possible - Store actual EXIF data for datetime extraction tests ## CI Pipeline Enhancements ### .github/workflows/ci.yml additions ```yaml # Add after existing unit-tests job e2e-tests: name: E2E Integration Tests needs: [shellcheck, syntax-check, unit-tests] runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 30 strategy: fail-fast: false matrix: platform: [icloud, dropbox, google-photos] steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Setup dependencies run: ... - name: Run ${{ matrix.platform }} test run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/${{ matrix.platform }}.test.sh env: GIIL_LOG_FORMAT: json ``` ## Local Development Scripts ### scripts/tests/run-all.sh ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash # Run all tests: unit + e2e echo '=== Unit Tests ===' ./scripts/tests/run-tests.sh echo '=== E2E Tests ===' for test in ./scripts/tests/e2e/*.test.sh; do echo "--- $(basename $test) ---" bash "$test" done ``` ### scripts/tests/run-quick.sh ```bash # Fast tests only (unit tests, skip live E2E) node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs ``` ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:01.784720427-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:07.109959354-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:07.109959354-05:00","close_reason":"Test infrastructure complete: lib/log.sh logging framework, run-all.sh and run-quick.sh convenience scripts, fixtures directories created. Commit b52b82c."} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-aqm","title":"Task: E2E tests for album mode (--all)","description":"## What Create E2E tests for album mode that downloads all photos from a shared album. ## Test Script scripts/tests/e2e/album-mode.test.sh ## Prerequisites - Need test albums with known photo counts: - iCloud album with 3 photos - Google Photos album with 3 photos ## Test Steps ```bash # Create temp output dir output_dir=$(mktemp -d) # Download album giil 'ALBUM_URL' --all --output "$output_dir" --json > output.json # Verify correct number of files count=$(ls "$output_dir"/*.jpg | wc -l) assert_equal "$count" 3 # Verify each file is valid image for f in "$output_dir"/*.jpg; do file "$f" | grep -q 'JPEG' || fail "Invalid image: $f" done ``` ## Verbose Logging ``` [TIME] [INFO] Album mode enabled [TIME] [INFO] URL: https://share.icloud.com/photos/... [TIME] [INFO] Detecting album structure... [TIME] [INFO] Found 3 photos in album [TIME] [INFO] Downloading 1/3... [TIME] [INFO] Downloading 2/3... [TIME] [INFO] Downloading 3/3... [TIME] [PASS] All 3 photos downloaded successfully ``` ## Edge Cases - Empty album (should exit gracefully) - Single-photo 'album' (should work like normal mode) - Very large album (test with limit if >10 photos) ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.138425981-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.138425981-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-aqm","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-fmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.139641299-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-aqm","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.141694354-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-ci6","title":"Task: E2E tests for output modes (--json, --base64, --preserve, --convert)","description":"## What Create E2E tests that verify all output mode flags work correctly. ## Test Script scripts/tests/e2e/output-modes.test.sh ## Output Modes to Test ### --json ```bash output=$(giil 'URL' --json) # Verify valid JSON echo "$output" | jq . # Verify required fields echo "$output" | jq -e '.ok and .schema_version and .platform and .path' ``` ### --base64 ```bash output=$(giil 'URL' --base64) # Verify valid base64 (decode should work) echo "$output" | base64 -d > /tmp/test.jpg file /tmp/test.jpg | grep -q 'JPEG' ``` ### --base64 --json (combined) ```bash output=$(giil 'URL' --base64 --json) # Verify JSON with base64 field instead of path echo "$output" | jq -e '.base64' ``` ### --preserve ```bash # Download with --preserve (skip MozJPEG) giil 'URL' --preserve --json # Verify file size matches original (larger than default compressed) ``` ### --convert webp ```bash giil 'URL' --convert webp --json # Verify output extension is .webp output=$(giil 'URL' --convert webp --json) echo "$output" | jq -r '.path' | grep -q '\.webp$' ``` ### --quality 50 ```bash # Download with lower quality giil 'URL' --quality 50 --json # Verify smaller file size ``` ## Verbose Logging Log file sizes to verify compression is working: ``` [TIME] [INFO] Testing output modes... [TIME] [INFO] Default (MozJPEG): 456 KB [TIME] [INFO] --preserve: 1.2 MB [TIME] [INFO] --quality 50: 234 KB [TIME] [INFO] --convert webp: 189 KB [TIME] [PASS] All output modes working ``` ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.078464993-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.078464993-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-ci6","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-fmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.107834271-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-ci6","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:22.113938262-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-emm","title":"Task: Unit tests for Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)","description":"## What Test extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl() function that transforms CDN URLs for full-resolution access. ## Function Behavior ```javascript // Input: https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=w1920-h1080 // Output: { baseUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx', fullResUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=s0' } ``` ## Test Cases 1. Standard CDN URL with size modifier (=w1920-h1080) 2. CDN URL with =s suffix (=s1920) 3. CDN URL without any modifier 4. Non-Google Photos URL (should return unchanged) 5. Empty string / null input 6. URL with query parameters after modifier ## File scripts/tests/google-photos.test.mjs ## Notes - The =s0 modifier requests maximum resolution - This is the key optimization for Google Photos full-res downloads ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:38.144996252-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:57.199867372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:57.199867372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 12 tests for extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl in google-photos-url.test.mjs. Commit da5318e.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-emm","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:38.173940441-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7","title":"EPIC: Phase 7 - Documentation & Release","description":"## Overview Comprehensive documentation updates and v3.0.0 release preparation. ## Documentation Updates ### 1. Rebrand README ```markdown

giil

Get Image [from] Internet Link

Originally built for iCloud, now supporting: - iCloud • Dropbox • Google Photos • Google Drive [experimental] ``` ### 2. Platform Support Table ```markdown | Platform | Method | Speed | Full Resolution | |----------|--------|-------|-----------------| | iCloud | Playwright (4-tier) | 5-15s | ✅ Yes | | Dropbox | Direct URL (no browser!) | 1-3s | ✅ Yes | | Google Photos | URL extraction + direct | 5-10s | ✅ Yes | | Google Drive | Playwright [experimental] | 5-15s | Conditional | ``` ### 3. Per-Platform Examples Show usage for each platform with expected output. ### 4. Per-Platform Troubleshooting - Dropbox: Password-protected, expired links - Google Photos: Private albums, video handling - Google Drive: "Anyone with link" requirement - iCloud: Existing troubleshooting (unchanged) ### 5. Migration Notes ```markdown ## Upgrading from v2.x Key changes: - Default: Original bytes preserved (compression now opt-in) - New JSON fields: schema_version, ok, platform - New exit codes: 10-13, 20 - New flags: --optimize, --convert, --print-url ``` ## AGENTS.md Updates - Add new platform architecture to "Supported Platforms" section - Update capture strategy diagram - Document new exit codes ## Release Checklist 1. [ ] Update VERSION to 3.0.0 2. [ ] Update README with all changes 3. [ ] Update AGENTS.md 4. [ ] Create release notes template 5. [ ] Tag v3.0.0 6. [ ] Verify GitHub release artifacts 7. [ ] Update install.sh URL in README ## Success Criteria - [ ] README reflects multi-platform support - [ ] Each platform has usage examples - [ ] Each platform has troubleshooting section - [ ] Migration notes help v2 users - [ ] v3.0.0 release published with checksums ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:26:24.290759855-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:50:05.125833974-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T02:50:05.125833974-05:00","close_reason":"All documentation subtasks complete: README expanded, AGENTS.md updated","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:26:24.320086429-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:27:23.958908937-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.1","title":"Rewrite README for multi-platform support","description":"## What Comprehensively update README.md to reflect multi-platform support, rebrand messaging, and v3 changes. ## Header Rebrand FROM: ```markdown # Get iCloud Image Link Download full-resolution images from iCloud photo shares ``` TO: ```markdown # giil ### Get Image [from] Internet Link Download full-resolution images from cloud sharing links. Originally built for iCloud, now supporting Dropbox, Google Photos, and Google Drive. ``` ## New Platform Support Table ```markdown ## Supported Platforms | Platform | Method | Speed | Full Resolution | Notes | |----------|--------|-------|-----------------|-------| | iCloud | Playwright (4-tier) | 5-15s | ✅ Always | Original and best-supported | | Dropbox | Direct URL | 1-3s | ✅ Always | No browser needed! | | Google Photos | URL extraction | 5-10s | ✅ Always | Uses =s0 modifier | | Google Drive | Playwright | 5-15s | Conditional | [Experimental] Requires public sharing | ``` ## Per-Platform Examples ```markdown ## Quick Examples ### iCloud \`\`\`bash giil "https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123" \`\`\` ### Dropbox (fastest!) \`\`\`bash giil "https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/xxx/photo.png?dl=0" # Direct download, no browser needed \`\`\` ### Google Photos \`\`\`bash giil "https://photos.app.goo.gl/abc123" # Also works with album links for --all mode \`\`\` ### Google Drive [experimental] \`\`\`bash giil "https://drive.google.com/file/d/xxx/view" # File must be shared as "Anyone with the link" \`\`\` ``` ## Per-Platform Troubleshooting ```markdown ## Troubleshooting ### Dropbox - **"Received HTML instead of image"**: Link may be password-protected or expired - **Empty output**: Verify the file is publicly shared ### Google Photos - **"AUTH_REQUIRED"**: Album is private. Ask owner to make it public. - **Low resolution**: Ensure giil is using =s0 modifier (check with --verbose) ### Google Drive - **"File is not publicly shared"**: Owner must set "Anyone with the link" access - **Quality warning**: Drive may return lower-resolution thumbnails as fallback ``` ## v3 Changes Section ```markdown ## Upgrading from v2.x ### Breaking Changes - **Exit codes 4 and 5 changed**: Network errors now exit 10, auth errors exit 11 - **JSON schema additions**: New fields `schema_version`, `ok`, `platform` ### Behavior Changes - **Original bytes preserved by default**: Use `--optimize` for MozJPEG compression - **New flags**: `--print-url`, `--verbose`, `--trace`, `--convert`, `--optimize` ### Migration \`\`\`bash # If you were checking exit codes: # v2: if [ $? -eq 4 ]; then → v3: if [ $? -eq 10 ]; then # v2: if [ $? -eq 5 ]; then → v3: if [ $? -eq 11 ]; then # If you relied on automatic JPEG compression: # v2: giil "https://..." # v3: giil "https://..." --optimize \`\`\` ``` ## File Size Considerations Current README is comprehensive. Be careful not to bloat it. Consider: - Keep main README focused on quick-start - Add PLATFORMS.md for deep platform-specific docs (if needed) - Link to GitHub wiki for edge cases ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:43:21.07736515-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:49:45.268462575-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T02:49:45.268462575-05:00","close_reason":"README was expanded with 225+ lines covering multi-platform support, JSON schema, exit codes, gum integration in previous session","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.1","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:43:21.106462934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.1","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T22:44:31.340184091-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.2","title":"Update AGENTS.md architecture diagram","description":"## What Update the architecture diagram in AGENTS.md to reflect v3 changes, specifically the Dropbox fast path that bypasses extractor.mjs. ## Current Diagram (INCORRECT for v3) ``` ┌─────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ │ Dropbox │ │ Google │ │ iCloud/ │ │ (curl) │ │ Photos │ │ GDrive │ └─────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ extractor.mjs (Playwright) │ ``` This shows Dropbox going to extractor.mjs, but v3 bypasses it entirely! ## Corrected Diagram (v3) ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ URL Input │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ ┌──────────────────┐ │ detect_platform()│ └──────────────────┘ │ ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ │ Dropbox │ │ Google │ │ iCloud/ │ │ FAST │ │ Photos │ │ GDrive │ └─────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ │ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ extractor.mjs (Playwright) │ │ │ │ │ │ TIER 1: Download button (9 selectors) │ │ │ ↓ fail │ │ │ TIER 2: Network CDN interception │ │ │ ↓ fail │ │ │ TIER 3: Element screenshot │ │ │ ↓ fail │ │ │ TIER 4: Viewport screenshot │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ curl │ │ Image Processing │ │ raw=1 │ │ Sharp + MozJPEG │ └─────────┘ │ EXIF extraction │ │ │ HEIC conversion │ │ └──────────────────┘ │ │ └───────────────┬───────────┘ ▼ ┌──────────────────┐ │ Output │ │ (file/json/b64) │ └──────────────────┘ ``` ## Key Changes to Highlight 1. **Dropbox exits early** - Direct curl download, no Playwright 2. **Platform-specific paths** - Each platform has tailored strategy 3. **Shared output layer** - All paths converge for final output ## Also Update 1. Exit codes table (add new codes 10-13, 20) 2. CLI options table (add new flags) 3. } log_info() { echo -e "${BLUE}[$(timestamp)] [INFO]${NC} $*" >&2; } log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[$(timestamp)] [WARN]${NC} $*" >&2; } log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[$(timestamp)] [PASS]${NC} $*" >&2 ((PASS_COUNT++)) } log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[$(timestamp)] [FAIL]${NC} $*" >&2 ((FAIL_COUNT++)) } # JSON mode (for CI) log_json() { local level=$1 msg=$2 echo "{\"ts\":\"$(timestamp)\",\"level\":\"$level\",\"msg\":\"$msg\"}" } # Summary at end of test test_summary() { echo "" echo "=== Test Summary ===" echo "Passed: $PASS_COUNT" echo "Failed: $FAIL_COUNT" if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then exit 1 fi } # Assertions assert_exit_code() { local expected=$1 actual=$2 msg=${3:-"Exit code check"} if [[ "$actual" == "$expected" ]]; then log_pass "$msg (exit $actual)" else log_fail "$msg: expected $expected, got $actual" fi } assert_file_exists() { local path=$1 if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then log_pass "File exists: $path" else log_fail "File missing: $path" fi } ``` ## Usage ```bash source scripts/tests/lib/log.sh log_info "Starting test..." log_pass "Download successful" log_fail "SHA mismatch" test_summary ``` ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:57.736795174-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:57.736795174-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-i8w","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:57.76274919-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-i8w","title":"Task: Create test logging framework (lib/log.sh)","description":"## What Create a shared logging library for all E2E test scripts with: - Timestamped log messages - Color-coded severity levels - JSON output mode for CI parsing - Pass/fail counters ## File scripts/tests/lib/log.sh ## Implementation ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash # Test logging library # Colors RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m' YELLOW='\033[0;33m' BLUE='\033[0;34m' NC='\033[0m' # No Color # Counters PASS_COUNT=0 FAIL_COUNT=0 timestamp() { date -Iseconds; } log_info() { echo -e "${BLUE}[$(timestamp)] [INFO]${NC} $*" >&2; } log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[$(timestamp)] [WARN]${NC} $*" >&2; } log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[$(timestamp)] [PASS]${NC} $*" >&2 ((PASS_COUNT++)) } log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[$(timestamp)] [FAIL]${NC} $*" >&2 ((FAIL_COUNT++)) } # JSON mode (for CI) log_json() { local level=$1 msg=$2 echo "{\"ts\":\"$(timestamp)\",\"level\":\"$level\",\"msg\":\"$msg\"}" } # Summary at end of test test_summary() { echo "" echo "=== Test Summary ===" echo "Passed: $PASS_COUNT" echo "Failed: $FAIL_COUNT" if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then exit 1 fi } # Assertions assert_exit_code() { local expected=$1 actual=$2 msg=${3:-"Exit code check"} if [[ "$actual" == "$expected" ]]; then log_pass "$msg (exit $actual)" else log_fail "$msg: expected $expected, got $actual" fi } assert_file_exists() { local path=$1 if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then log_pass "File exists: $path" else log_fail "File missing: $path" fi } ``` ## Usage ```bash source scripts/tests/lib/log.sh log_info "Starting test..." log_pass "Download successful" log_fail "SHA mismatch" test_summary ``` ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:57.736795174-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:08.111067391-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:08.111067391-05:00","close_reason":"Logging framework created at scripts/tests/lib/log.sh with JSON/text modes, timing, test results, suite summaries.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-i8w","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:43:57.76274919-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-jrg","title":"Task: Unit tests for filename generation (generateFilename)","description":"## What Test generateFilename() function that creates output filenames from platform, datetime, and index. ## Function Behavior ```javascript // Platform + datetime: icloud_20240115_143245.jpg // Platform + index: icloud_001.jpg // Platform only: icloud_.jpg ``` ## Test Cases 1. With valid datetime and no index 2. With index and no datetime 3. With both datetime and index 4. With neither (falls back to current timestamp) 5. Different platforms (icloud, dropbox, gphotos, gdrive) 6. Different extensions (jpg, png, webp) 7. Edge case: datetime with timezone 8. Edge case: very large index ## File scripts/tests/filename-generation.test.mjs ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:59.789336649-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:59.789336649-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-jrg","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:59.819146127-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-m4d","title":"Task: Unit tests for bash functions (detect_platform, normalize_url)","description":"## What Create unit tests for pure bash functions in giil script. ## Approach Use bats-core (Bash Automated Testing System) for bash function testing. ## Functions to Test ### detect_platform (bash version) ```bash @test 'detect_platform returns icloud for share.icloud.com' { result=$(detect_platform 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123') [ "$result" = 'icloud' ] } @test 'detect_platform returns dropbox for dropbox.com/s/' { result=$(detect_platform 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc123/photo.jpg') [ "$result" = 'dropbox' ] } ``` ### normalize_url ```bash @test 'normalize_url converts icloud.com/photos# to share.icloud.com' { result=$(normalize_url 'https://www.icloud.com/photos/#abc123') [ "$result" = 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123' ] } ``` ### normalize_dropbox_url ```bash @test 'normalize_dropbox_url adds raw=1' { result=$(normalize_dropbox_url 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc/photo.jpg?dl=0') [[ "$result" == *'raw=1'* ]] } ``` ## Setup 1. Install bats-core: `npm install -g bats` 2. Create scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats 3. Source giil functions for testing ## File scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats ## Notes - Bats is widely used for bash testing - Tests run in isolated subshells - Supports setup/teardown hooks ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:44:26.437927108-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:44:26.437927108-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-m4d","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:44:26.439208341-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-qoh","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage for giil v3.0","description":"## Overview giil currently has minimal test coverage: - 2 unit test files (platform-detection, content-validation) - 1 integration test (iCloud only, no Dropbox/Google Photos/Google Drive) - No error scenario tests - No output mode tests (--base64, --preserve, --convert) - No verbose logging in test infrastructure ## Goal Achieve comprehensive test coverage using REAL tests (no mocks/fakes) with detailed logging for debugging. All tests should: 1. Test actual functionality against real or realistic data 2. Provide verbose, structured logging for CI debugging 3. Cover all code paths and edge cases 4. Support both local development and CI execution ## Major Gaps to Fill ### Unit Tests (Pure Functions) - URL normalization (normalize_url, normalize_dropbox_url) - EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime) - Filename generation (generateFilename) - JSON output formatting (formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError) - Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl) - Exit code error classification - processAndSaveImage logic branches ### Integration Tests (E2E) - Multi-platform: iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, Google Drive - Error scenarios: timeout, auth required, not found, unsupported type - Output modes: --json, --base64, --preserve, --convert - Album mode: --all for different platforms - CLI flags: --verbose, --trace, --print-url, --debug-dir ### Bash Layer Tests - CLI argument parsing - Platform detection (bash version) - Dependency detection - Version consistency ### Infrastructure - Verbose structured logging (JSON format for CI parsing) - Test fixtures with real image files - Replay tests for deterministic results ## Success Criteria - 90%+ code coverage on testable pure functions - All 4 platforms have at least 1 live integration test - All exit codes have corresponding error scenario tests - All CLI flags have at least 1 test - Detailed logging makes CI failures debuggable ","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:39:47.940248927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:44:43.988915001-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-qoh","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:45:47.498285668-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
